intent: A validation function evaluates against the working frame as of its position in the pipeline (seeing columns/rows produced by upstream built-in steps), instead of always the original source instance table (issue #8).
affected_components: backend/domain/runner/executors.py (validation frame selection, evolve/report split, identity guard)
dependencies: resolve_frame RAW/TRANSFORMED seam (from runner-resolution-model) — natural reuse point for SQL-validation materialization
assumptions: Scoping (issue #8 comment): identity classification — filter/rename/transforms preserve source-record identity; join/pivot break it.
Scoping: a validation gives the same verdict regardless of run mode (consistent-everywhere); set-mode evolves the full upstream pipeline.

resolved_decisions.question: Semantic model for validations after built-ins (from #8 scoping)
resolved_decisions.resolution: Option C — identity-preserving: validations evaluate against the working frame at their position when upstream steps preserve source-record identity; after a join/pivot a validation reports a new 'not_applicable' status rather than misreporting failing_rows. Chosen over A (always position-aware, failing_rows undefined post-reshape) and B (per-step opt-in flag) in the #8 scoping session.

resolved_decisions.question: Is SQL-validation frame materialization in the MVP, or a fast-follow?
resolved_decisions.resolution: In the MVP. 'Consistent everywhere' was the explicit goal; shipping pandas-only would silently recreate the pandas-vs-SQL inconsistency that was just rejected. So SQL validations must also evaluate against the position working frame in v1.

resolved_decisions.question: Mechanism for making SQL validations see the working frame at their position?
resolved_decisions.resolution: Register the in-memory working frame as a transient DuckDB view per SQL validation and rewrite {source_table} to it (mechanism (a)); unregister after. Chosen over pointing at the persisted staging table (b) because staging is suppressed in validation-only runs and reflects the latest step, not the validation's position. No reliance on the instance table being a real persisted table (no index/constraint dependence), so a view suffices — no CREATE TEMP TABLE copy. Requires execute_sql_function to accept a target table/view name instead of always substituting the instance table.

open_risks_to_resolve: NEXT QUESTION (Q3, paused): not_applicable trigger granularity — conservative latch (any validation at/after the first join/pivot is not_applicable) vs per-validation column analysis vs an opt-in 'validate reshaped frame anyway' escape hatch. Recommendation on the table: conservative latch for v1.
SQL-validation mechanism RESOLVED (registered view); remaining: execute_sql_function needs a target-table param (impl detail).
execute-vs-report decoupling correctness: every run mode evolves the frame (transforms+built-ins) but reports per run_type; staging suppressed in validation-only runs.
Performance: run_validation_across_sources now runs each source's full transform+built-in pipeline (heavier).
not_applicable result-model + frontend: how the new status flows through ValidationRunResult.to_dict and is rendered (still open).
